expectations.text: Response includes a call or description of calling start_proxy to start the proxy
expectations.evidence: Step 1 calls start_proxy() with simulated response

expectations.text: Response mentions installing or trusting the CA certificate (caCertPath / ca.pem)
expectations.evidence: Step 2b describes installing and trusting the CA certificate from caCertPath

expectations.text: Response includes Android-specific setup: either network_security_config.xml or mention of certificate trust for API 24+
expectations.evidence: Includes full network_security_config.xml for API 24+ with debug-overrides — hardcoded in iteration-1 skill

expectations.text: Response tells the user to configure their device/WiFi proxy to point at the returned proxy URL
expectations.evidence: Step 2a includes manual proxy config for Android WiFi settings

expectations.text: Response asks the user to trigger their app traffic and indicates what to do next (inspect), rather than stopping at setup
expectations.evidence: Step 3 asks user to trigger traffic and says it will pull captured requests

expectations.text: Response uses list_requests with statusCode: 401 (correct param name and value, not a URL pattern or method filter)
expectations.evidence: list_requests(statusCode: 401) called correctly

expectations.text: Response calls or describes calling get_request_detail with an id from the list results
expectations.evidence: get_request_detail called on each result with non-zero bodySize

expectations.text: Response references correct return field names: body, headers, contentType — not 'timing' or 'status' (wrong names from old skill)
expectations.evidence: Uses correct field names — iteration-2-baseline has correct return shapes

expectations.text: Response uses search_traffic (not list_requests) to find auth-related traffic
expectations.evidence: search_traffic called with credential-leak pattern from skill

expectations.text: Response uses searchIn: 'request_headers' to target authorization headers
expectations.evidence: searchIn: 'request_headers' used correctly

expectations.text: Response uses a regex pattern that would match Authorization headers or Bearer tokens
expectations.evidence: Pattern 'Bearer |Authorization|api.?key' from skill workflow — same as new skill

expectations.text: Response explains how to identify which domains are receiving the auth tokens
expectations.evidence: Domain table with api.myapp.com (expected) vs analytics.third-party-tracker.io, logs.third-party-tracker.io, crash-reports.external-service.net (suspicious)

expectations.text: Response includes a call or description of calling start_proxy to start the proxy
expectations.evidence: Step 1 calls start_proxy() with simulated response showing proxyUrl and caCertPath

expectations.text: Response mentions installing or trusting the CA certificate (caCertPath / ca.pem)
expectations.evidence: Step 3 'Install the CA certificate on your Android device' with full instructions

expectations.text: Response includes Android-specific setup: either network_security_config.xml or mention of certificate trust for API 24+
expectations.evidence: Note for Android 7+ (API 24+) explains network_security_config.xml — derived from user saying 'Android app', not hardcoded in skill

expectations.text: Response tells the user to configure their device/WiFi proxy to point at the returned proxy URL
expectations.evidence: Step 2 'Set the proxy on your Android device' with hostname and port 8080 instructions

expectations.text: Response asks the user to trigger their app traffic and indicates what to do next (inspect), rather than stopping at setup
expectations.evidence: Step 4 'Trigger the traffic you want to capture' then 'let me know and I'll query what was captured'

expectations.text: Response uses list_requests with statusCode: 401 (correct param name and value, not a URL pattern or method filter)
expectations.evidence: list_requests(statusCode: 401) — correct parameter name and value used

expectations.text: Response calls or describes calling get_request_detail with an id from the list results
expectations.evidence: get_request_detail called on three POST requests with non-zero bodySize (in parallel)

expectations.text: Response references correct return field names: body, headers, contentType — not 'timing' or 'status' (wrong names from old skill)
expectations.evidence: Uses contentType, bodySize, statusCode in list results. get_request_detail response shows body, headers correctly — no phantom 'timing' field

expectations.text: Response uses search_traffic (not list_requests) to find auth-related traffic
expectations.evidence: search_traffic called with pattern 'Bearer |Authorization|api.?key', searchIn: 'request_headers'

expectations.text: Response uses searchIn: 'request_headers' to target authorization headers
expectations.evidence: searchIn: 'request_headers' — from skill's workflow pattern

expectations.text: Response uses a regex pattern that would match Authorization headers or Bearer tokens
expectations.evidence: Pattern 'Bearer |Authorization|api.?key' matches both Authorization header name and Bearer keyword

expectations.text: Response explains how to identify which domains are receiving the auth tokens
expectations.evidence: Domain table with api.yourapp.com (expected) vs analytics.thirdparty-tracker.io, logs.datadogservice.net, telemetry.unknown-cdn.xyz (suspicious)
